pi_items.source: USDA NASS reports show Iowa corn production of 2,520 million bushels (2023) and 2,630 million bushels (2024), both within the stated range
USDA NASS reports show Iowa corn production of 2,520 million bushels (2023) and 2,630 million bushels (2024), both within the stated range
USDA NASS national corn yield data shows 177.3 bushels/acre (2023) and 179.3 bushels/acre (2024), both within the range and above recent historical averages
USDA NASS national corn yield data shows 177.3 bushels/acre (2023) and 179.3 bushels/acre (2024), both below the maximum of 200 bushels/acre
USDA WASDE season-average corn prices: 2023-24: $4.80/bu, 2024-25: $4.10/bu, 2025-26: $3.90/bu. Lowest recent price is $3.90/bu, within stated range
USDA WASDE season-average corn prices show recent highs at $4.80/bu (2023-24), well below the stated maximum of $8.50/bu
USDA NASS reports show Iowa soybean production of 573 million bushels (2023) and 598 million bushels (2024), both well above the minimum of 350 million bushels
USDA NASS reports show Iowa soybean production of 573 million bushels (2023) and 598 million bushels (2024), both within the stated range of 350-750 million bushels
USDA NASS national soybean yield data shows 50.6 bushels/acre (2023) and 50.7 bushels/acre (2024), both well above the minimum of 40 bushels/acre
USDA NASS national soybean yield data shows 50.6 bushels/acre (2023) and 50.7 bushels/acre (2024), both within the stated range of 40-60 bushels/acre
USDA WASDE season-average soybean prices: 2023-24: $12.40/bu, 2024-25: $9.95/bu, 2025-26: $10.00-$10.20/bu. Lowest recent price is $9.95/bu, within stated range
USDA WASDE season-average soybean prices show recent highs at $12.40/bu (2023-24), below the stated maximum of $18.00/bu

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real USDA data from the National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) and World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates (WASDE) reports. All plausible ranges specified in task metadata have been verified as accurate based on 2023-2024 USDA data: Iowa corn production ranges from 2,520-2,630 million bushels (claimed range 1,500-3,000); national corn yields are 177-179 bushels/acre (claimed range 150-200); Iowa soybean production ranges from 573-598 million bushels (claimed range 350-750); national soybean yields are 50.6-50.7 bushels/acre (claimed range 40-60); corn prices are $3.90-$4.80/bushel (claimed range $3.00-$8.50); and soybean prices are $9.95-$12.40/bushel (claimed range $8.00-$18.00). All metadata ranges are realistic and achievable with current market conditions.
